title: Decompose PlayerAccount into separate persistence aggregates
body:
## Issue description

`PostgresPlayerRepository.findById()` performs a single query that JOINs 5-6 tables (`players`, `player_run_state`, `player_meta_progression`, `player_idempotency_keys`, `player_reward_claims`) and reconstructs the entire `PlayerAccount` aggregate. Similarly, `save()` updates all sub-tables in a transaction even when only one sub-aggregate changed. This monolithic approach creates:

- **Read amplification:** Every command loads everything, even if it only needs `RunState`.
- **Write contention:** All writes touch the same row set, increasing lock contention under concurrent requests.
- **Coupling:** Adding new sub-features (research, achievements, inventory) means expanding the already-complex JOIN.

### Current state
- `PlayerAccount` is the single root aggregate containing `RunState`, `MetaProgression`, `RewardClaim[]`, and `processedIdempotencyKeys[]`.
- One `PlayerRepository` port with `findById()`, `create()`, `save()`.
- `save()` updates `players`, `player_run_state`, `player_meta_progression`, upserts idempotency keys, and upserts reward claims — all in one transaction.

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. Introduce separate repository ports for independent sub-aggregates:
   - `RunStateRepository` — manages `player_run_state` independently.
   - `MetaProgressionRepository` — manages `player_meta_progression` independently.
   - Keep `PlayerRepository` for the root `players` table and cross-cutting concerns.
2. Each sub-repository has its own `findByPlayerId()` and `save()` with optimistic concurrency.
3. Command handlers load only the sub-aggregates they need:
   - `BuyUpgrade` needs `RunState` only.
   - `ClaimOfflineEarnings` needs `RunState` + `MetaProgression`.
   - `PrestigeReset` needs `RunState` + `MetaProgression`.
4. The full `PlayerAccount` reconstruction (for `GET /players/:playerId`) becomes a query-side concern, not a command-side requirement.
5. Add `version` columns to `player_run_state` and `player_meta_progression` for independent optimistic concurrency.

## Additional notes

- **Sequencing:** Immediate. Unblocks cleaner command handlers and read model work.
- **Dependencies:** None — can be done in parallel with idempotency results.
- **Affected files:**
  - `packages/application/src/ports/PlayerRepository.ts` (refactor)
  - `packages/application/src/ports/` (new: `RunStateRepository.ts`, `MetaProgressionRepository.ts`)
  - `packages/infrastructure/src/postgres/PostgresPlayerRepository.ts` (split)
  - `packages/infrastructure/src/postgres/migrations/` (new migration for version columns)
  - All command handlers in `packages/application/src/commands/`
  - `apps/api/src/routes/index.ts` (GET endpoint reconstruction)
  - `apps/api/src/config/repositories.ts` (wire new repos)
  - `apps/worker/src/index.ts` (wire new repos)
- Maintain backward compatibility — the existing `PlayerAccount` type can remain as a composite read DTO.
- Consider doing this in two PRs: (1) add version columns + new ports, (2) migrate command handlers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `RunStateRepository` and `MetaProgressionRepository` ports exist with `findByPlayerId()` and `save()`
- [ ] Each sub-aggregate table has its own `version` column for optimistic concurrency
- [ ] Command handlers load only the sub-aggregates they need
- [ ] `GET /players/:playerId` still returns the full player state (reconstructed from sub-repos or a query)
- [ ] Optimistic concurrency is maintained per sub-aggregate
- [ ] New migration adds version columns to sub-tables
- [ ] All existing tests pass (with updated mocks)
- [ ] New tests verify independent sub-aggregate loading and saving
